content: I'm currently looking into addressing the feedback on my LED patchseries, and while rewriting mine to use the other Resource abstraction I found ioport_map and hence iowrite*. Now I wonder why the Io Struct from here uses write* which crashes with memory that has the portmem cookie and not iowrite* which does sound more fitting for something called Io

One trivial idea to still support both would be to maybe add a generic const bool enabling iowrite and also another one to enable the readb and others. This would allow me to use the same abstraction for my port memory region abstraction, but still do not offer apis that will crash the kernel

Fiona Behrens said:

I'm currently looking into addressing the feedback on my LED patchseries, and while rewriting mine to use the other Resource abstraction I found ioport_map and hence iowrite*. Now I wonder why the Io Struct from here uses write* which crashes with memory that has the portmem cookie and not iowrite* which does sound more fitting for something called Io

I guess the reason is that no one really needs to deal with a port IO device? ;-) So mmio abstraction is good enough?
Well I use port IO for the SE10 led driver as the LED there is via portmemory mapped access
I must defer the technical bits to Danilo and others, but in general we should cover port IO in Resource and Io abstraction.
(have a somewhat working abstraction here, but it's still WIP https://cyberchaos.dev/kloenk/linux/-/blob/kloenk/rust/leds-v3/rust/kernel/io/port.rs?ref_type=heads)

Had a new idea, currently preparing to write a RFC, but also can outline my idea here alrady.
Idea is to add a const generic HAS_DIRRECT_ACCESS (name is not at all final) to the io struct. Drivers that know that it can be access with writeb can then just set that to true. iowrite will always be available as for non portb it uses the same cookie as far as I understand. And then I add a second struct (IoPort) that adds the outb functions (which uses a different cookie than the same iowrite8 would need

While doing that change I notice that the pci abstraction is therefore wrong to the documentation. This says you are suposed to use iowrite/ioread as this can be MMIO or PIO (https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v6.13/source/drivers/pci/iomap.c#L110)
I see that other drivers also use write* here, but should we properly forbid that with the rust type system?
